title: E-Visa recommendation · BIDA
description: Coordination of the BIDA E-Visa recommendation letter. The prerequisite for the foreign national's E-Visa endorsement at a Bangladesh mission abroad.
workflow:
Process: Document collection and pre-validation → BIDA OSS portal submission → BIDA review and inter-ministerial routing → Recommendation letter issuance.

Engagement covers: Handling typically one to two rounds of deficiency queries on board resolution wording, tax clearance currency, and inward remittance proof; refreshing documents that age past validity windows during review; coordinating with the company secretary on board resolution corrections; and managing the BIDA inter-ministerial committee schedule, which meets twice monthly.
notes:
Authority: Bangladesh Investment Development Authority (BIDA) · Work Permit Cell · OSS Portal (bidaquickserv.org)
Legal basis: BIDA Act, 2016; Foreigners Act, 1946; Work Permit Guideline, 2011 (as periodically updated)
Notes: Issuance subject to authority's review and discretion.

title: E-Visa · Category change
description: Application coordination for visa category conversion, typically from VOA / Arrival visa to an E-Visa, with the relevant immigration authority.
workflow:
Process: Visa document collection → Immigration application → Status tracking → Category conversion approval.

Engagement covers: Presenting the case before the Visa Section officer when the document trail raises queries on the applicant's prior visa category or stay duration; managing re-interview requests; and follow-up filing where the officer asks for additional employer or sponsor documents.
notes:
Authority: Department of Immigration and Passports (DIP) · Visa Wing · Agargaon HQ
Legal basis: Foreigners Act, 1946; Foreigners Order, 1951; Visa Policy, 2014 (Ministry of Home Affairs)
Notes: Outcome depends on the applicant's existing visa status and supporting documentation.

title: E-Visa · 3-month entry
description: Coordination of the 3-month single or multiple-entry E-Visa application, typically used before work permit issuance.
deliverables: Document preparation and notarised attestation by company
workflow:
Process: Document preparation and notarised attestation by company → Mission or DIP submission → Processing → Visa endorsement.

Engagement covers: Liaising with the issuing Bangladesh mission abroad for incomplete document queries; scheduling embassy appointments where mandatory; document re-attestation where the mission flags inconsistencies between board resolution, appointment letter, and passport details; and managing time-zone driven communication lag with overseas missions.
notes:
Authority: Issuing Bangladesh High Commission or Embassy (per applicant's country of origin)
Legal basis: Foreigners Act, 1946; Foreigners Order, 1951; Visa Policy, 2014
Notes: Issuance subject to the issuing mission's review and consular discretion.

title: Work permit · New application
description: End to end coordination for a new work permit through BIDA OSS. Packaging, fee payment, and inter-ministerial follow up.
deliverables: Employer documentation prep and notarised attestation
BIDA OSS submission within 15 days of arrival
workflow:
Process: Employer documentation prep and notarised attestation → BIDA OSS submission within 15 days of arrival → Inter-ministerial committee review → Security clearance coordination → Permit issuance.

Engagement covers: Managing the strict 15-day post-arrival submission deadline; one to two rounds of BIDA deficiency queries on local advertisement evidence, manpower statement, and remuneration justification; refreshing tax clearance and trade license if review extends past 30/60-day validity windows; coordinating between BIDA, MoHA, NSI, and SB during the inter-ministerial process; and aligning submission timing against the inter-ministerial committee's twice-monthly schedule.
notes:
Authority: BIDA · Work Permit Cell · OSS Portal (bidaquickserv.org)
Legal basis: BIDA Act, 2016; Foreigners Act, 1946; Work Permit Guideline, 2011
Notes: BIDA government fee: BDT 25,000 + 15% VAT (online). Typically valid for one year.

title: E-Visa · 1-year multiple entry
description: Coordination of the 1-year multiple-entry E-Visa endorsement against an issued work permit.
workflow:
Process: Work permit verification → Immigration application → Processing → Visa endorsement.

Engagement covers: Cross-referencing work permit and visa records before submission to catch discrepancies; managing DIP queries on prior visa history and entry-exit log; and re-submission where the work permit copy is rejected on technical grounds such as scan quality or signature visibility.
notes:
Authority: Department of Immigration and Passports (DIP) · Visa Wing · Agargaon HQ
Legal basis: Foreigners Act, 1946; Foreigners Order, 1951; Visa Policy, 2014
Notes: Requires a valid work permit as prerequisite.

title: E-Visa · Extension (MoHA)
description: Preparation and filing of E-Visa extension applications with the Ministry of Home Affairs, timed against contract and permit windows.
workflow:
Process: Extension document prep → MoHA Visa Section submission → Tracking → Approval issuance.

Engagement covers: Handling the MoHA Visa Cell officer's query on continued employment justification (commonly raised); refreshing the company NoC, tax records, and BIDA endorsement if the file is queued past its validity window; and managing the 30-day pre-expiry submission discipline to avoid overstay penalties.
notes:
Authority: Ministry of Home Affairs (MoHA) · Public Security Division · Visa Section
Legal basis: Foreigners Act, 1946; Foreigners Order, 1951; Visa Policy, 2014
Notes: Best initiated 30 days before current visa expiry.

title: Expatriate TIN · Registration
description: Online TIN registration coordination for individual expatriates, via the NBR e-TIN portal directly or BIDA OSS integration.
workflow:
Process: Passport and personal data collection → e-TIN portal application → Verification → Certificate generation.

Engagement covers: Managing OTP and email verification cycles; correcting portal entry errors that block submission; and re-submission where the system rejects passport details on character transliteration mismatches (a common issue with non-Latin script names).
notes:
Authority: National Board of Revenue (NBR) · e-TIN Portal (etaxnbr.gov.bd)
Legal basis: Income Tax Act, 2023 (replaced the 1984 Ordinance); latest amended through Finance Ordinance, 2025
Notes: Mandatory for expatriates earning income in Bangladesh.

title: Security clearance · MoHA / NSI / SB
description: Document submission and follow up coordination for the security clearance step alongside the work permit. Processed through SSD with NSI and Special Branch inputs.
deliverables: Security vetting by NSI and SB (parallel tracks)
workflow:
Process: Document submission → Security vetting by NSI and SB (parallel tracks) → Follow-up with SSD → Clearance issuance.

Engagement covers: Managing multi-agency throughput where NSI and SB operate on independent timelines that cannot be coordinated externally; refreshing employer NoC and supporting documents if vetting extends past validity; keeping the file active to prevent archival for inactivity (a real risk on long-pending cases); and where the first round returns an adverse note, presenting the case for re-vetting with additional employer or character references.
notes:
Authority: MoHA · Security Services Division (SSD), coordinating with NSI and Special Branch (SB)
Legal basis: Foreigners Act, 1946; Foreigners Order, 1951; Visa Policy, 2014; SSD internal directives
Notes: Multi-agency vetting. Timeline depends on agency throughput and cannot be accelerated.

title: Work permit · Extension
description: Renewal filing of an existing work permit before expiry, with updated documentation packaged for BIDA OSS.
deliverables: Updated tax clearance, trade licence, tax-return proof, manpower statement
workflow:
Process: Existing permit review → Updated tax clearance, trade licence, tax-return proof, manpower statement → BIDA OSS submission → Approval.

Engagement covers: Handling BIDA renewal review queries (typically more frequent than first issuance); refreshing tax-return proof and trade licence copies that age out during review; and presenting written justification where the sponsor entity or commercial reality has shifted since first issuance (a common renewal trigger for inter-ministerial re-review).
notes:
Authority: BIDA · Work Permit Cell · OSS Portal
Legal basis: BIDA Act, 2016; Foreigners Act, 1946; Work Permit Guideline, 2011
Notes: Renewal review may scrutinise alignment between sponsor entity and original approval rationale.

title: Work permit · Amendment
description: Application coordination for amendments to an issued work permit. Role, salary, or sponsor changes.
workflow:
Process: Change request preparation → BIDA OSS submission → Review → Amendment approval.

Engagement covers: Handling BIDA's threshold query on whether the change is material (which would trigger a fresh review rather than amendment); preparing the supporting justification that keeps the case on the amendment track; and coordinating with HR for revised employment letter or board resolution where required.
notes:
Authority: BIDA · Work Permit Cell · OSS Portal
Legal basis: BIDA Act, 2016; Foreigners Act, 1946; Work Permit Guideline, 2011
Notes: Material role or sponsor changes may trigger re-evaluation rather than a simple amendment.

title: Work permit · Cancellation
description: Filing for cancellation of a work permit on separation, repatriation, or transfer.
workflow:
Process: Cancellation application filing → BIDA review → Cancellation acknowledgment.

Engagement covers: Ensuring the expatriate's tax file closing and exit-tax clearance are progressed in parallel (without which the foreign national cannot legally exit); coordinating final settlement documentation; and securing the cancellation acknowledgment letter that the airline and DIP require at departure.
notes:
Authority: BIDA · Work Permit Cell · OSS Portal
Legal basis: BIDA Act, 2016; Foreigners Act, 1946; Work Permit Guideline, 2011
Notes: Required for clean tax file closing and onward immigration.

title: Expatriate tax · Filing & certificate
description: Annual income-tax return filing for the expatriate with NBR, with tax clearance certificate issuance support.
workflow:
Process: Financial data collection → Return preparation → NBR filing → Tax clearance certificate.

Engagement covers: Handling DC queries on income computation, foreign-income treatment, and exempt-income claims; managing requests for additional source proof (commonly remittance statements and employment certificates); and obtaining the tax clearance certificate as a separate step after assessment, since filing alone does not produce the certificate.
notes:
Authority: NBR · Taxes Zone and Circle (assigned per applicant's TIN)
Legal basis: Income Tax Act, 2023 (latest amended by Finance Ordinance, 2025); Travel Tax Act, 2003
Notes: Tax clearance certificate is required for visa renewal and onward travel.

title: Expatriate tax · File closing
description: Final tax reconciliation and file closure with NBR on permanent departure.
workflow:
Process: Final tax reconciliation → NBR submission → File closure certificate.

Engagement covers: Obtaining a formal no-objection from the Taxes Zone; settling any outstanding TDS gaps before closure; and securing the formal file closure letter that BIDA requires for work permit cancellation and that airlines may require at departure.
notes:
Authority: NBR · Taxes Zone and Circle (assigned per applicant's TIN)
Legal basis: Income Tax Act, 2023 (latest amended by Finance Ordinance, 2025); Travel Tax Act, 2003
Notes: Required when expatriate leaves Bangladesh permanently.

title: HR consultancy
description: Project-based HR advisory covering org design, policy frameworks, performance systems, and people strategy.
workflow:
Process: Diagnostic → Recommendations → Implementation support → Handover.

Engagement covers: Stakeholder interviews across leadership and HR; documented recommendations with implementation timelines and ownership; and a structured handover document for the client's internal team that holds beyond the engagement window.
notes:
Authority: LLP Advisory Practice (internal delivery)
Legal basis: Not a statutory engagement; governed by signed scope and MSA
Notes: Scope-dependent; proposed pricing pending sign-off.

title: Compensation & benefits survey
description: Industry and role-level compensation benchmarking with custom benefit-design recommendations.
deliverables: Data collection from sources and peers
workflow:
Process: Scope definition → Data collection from sources and peers → Analysis → Benchmark report → Recommendations.

Engagement covers: Confidentiality arrangements with peer firms participating in the benchmark; role-mapping where peer designations differ from the client's; and benefit-design recommendations that go beyond raw compensation comparison.
notes:
Authority: LLP Advisory Practice (internal delivery)
Legal basis: Not a statutory engagement; governed by signed scope and MSA
Notes: Depth and peer count drive final fee.

title: Labour law compliance · Advisory & audit
description: Structured audit of HR practices and statutory filings against the Labour Act 2006 and Labour Rules 2015, with a gap report and remediation plan.
workflow:
Process: Document review → Onsite walk-through → Statutory filing verification → Gap report → Remediation roadmap.

Engagement covers: Identification of unfiled annual returns, deficient registers, and exposure points that surface in DIFE inspections; review of disciplinary files against BLA standards for show-cause, enquiry, and termination procedure; and Labour-Court precedent risk mapping. Real-world calls include separating theoretical compliance gaps from those that actually surface in DIFE audits, and prioritising the remediation roadmap by exposure rather than alphabetical order.
notes:
Authority: LLP Compliance Practice · against DIFE filing standards and Labour Court precedent
Legal basis: Bangladesh Labour Act, 2006 (latest amended by BLA Amendment Act, 2026); Bangladesh Labour Rules, 2015 (latest amended 2022)
Notes: Renamed from 'Legal (Labor Law) Compliance & Licensing Services' to avoid overlap with Statutory Licensing.

title: Other HR & administrative services
description: Custom HR and administrative work outside the standard catalog, scoped on request.
workflow:
Process: Need conversation → Written scope → Fixed-fee quote → Delivery.

Engagement covers: Up-front feasibility check before commercial commitment, written scope document binding both sides, and clear handover at completion.
notes:
Authority: LLP Service Desk (internal delivery)
Legal basis: Determined at scoping
Notes: Catch-all entry for non-standard requests.

title: Trade license · New (City Corporation)
description: End-to-end coordination for a new trade licence from a City Corporation, including documentation, fee deposit, and zonal office submission.
workflow:
Process: Document prep → Zonal office submission → Holding-tax verification → License issuance.

Engagement covers: Zonal officer field verification (commonly required for first-time addresses); clearing any outstanding holding tax on the premises (a blocker that internal teams often discover too late); and one to two rounds of office query on business category classification, since rate scales differ by category.
notes:
Authority: DNCC or DSCC · Zonal Tax Office · e-Trade Portal (etradelicense.gov.bd) where available, or applicable Pourashava
Legal basis: Local Government (City Corporation) Act, 2009; Local Government (Pourashava) Act, 2009; relevant City Corporation Trade License By-laws
Notes: Government licence fee varies BDT 100–40,000 by business type and paid-up capital.

title: Trade license · Renewal (City Corporation)
description: Annual renewal filing with the City Corporation. Via online portals (DNCC/DSCC e-trade) or zonal offices.
workflow:
Process: License review → Document update → City Corp submission → Renewal issuance.

Engagement covers: Clearance of holding-tax dues that would otherwise block renewal; managing the 30 June fiscal-year deadline and avoiding the penalty regime; and coordinating where the e-trade portal fails to recognise prior licence details (a known DSCC area issue).
notes:
Authority: DNCC or DSCC · Zonal Tax Office · e-Trade Portal
Legal basis: Local Government (City Corporation) Act, 2009; Pourashava Act, 2009; Trade License By-laws
Notes: Must be renewed annually by 30 June; late renewal incurs penalty.

title: Trade license · Renewal (Factory)
description: Annual renewal filing for factory-located businesses with the relevant Pourashava or zonal office.
workflow:
Process: Documentation → Municipal submission → Renewal approval.

Engagement covers: Pourashava inspection where required; zoning verification for industrial use; and ensuring no environmental or fire compliance flags from upstream authorities are blocking renewal downstream.
notes:
Authority: Pourashava or Union Parishad (per project location); or City Corp Zonal Office
Legal basis: Local Government (City Corporation) Act, 2009; Pourashava Act, 2009; Trade License By-laws
Notes: Factory location adds inspection and zoning verification steps.

title: Company TIN certificate
description: Online TIN certificate registration for newly incorporated or amending companies through the NBR e-TIN portal.
workflow:
Process: Document collection → e-TIN portal application → Verification → Certificate issuance.

Engagement covers: Portal-side verification rounds; character-mismatch corrections where entity-name spelling diverges between RJSC and applicant inputs; and ensuring the entity name and registered address match RJSC records exactly (any mismatch triggers automatic rejection).
notes:
Authority: NBR · e-TIN Portal (etaxnbr.gov.bd)
Legal basis: Income Tax Act, 2023; latest amended through Finance Ordinance, 2025
Notes: Required for VAT BIN, trade license, and bank operations.

title: VAT BIN registration
description: BIN (Business Identification Number) registration with NBR VAT Wing for companies above the turnover threshold.
workflow:
Process: Document collection → NBR BIN portal application → Verification → Certificate issuance.

Engagement covers: Handling NBR VAT-wing queries on business-activity classification, premises proof, and turnover estimation; managing BIN-circle assignment based on geography; and ensuring downstream VAT registration aligns with how the business actually operates (a misalignment surfaces in the first VAT return cycle).
notes:
Authority: NBR · VAT Online Portal (vat.gov.bd)
Legal basis: VAT and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012; VAT and Supplementary Duty Rules, 2016; latest amended through Finance Ordinance, 2025
Notes: Mandatory for companies exceeding BDT 30 lakh turnover threshold.

title: Company registration · RJSC
description: Private Limited Company registration with the Registrar of Joint Stock Companies and Firms. Includes CoI, MoA, and AoA drafting.
workflow:
Process: Name clearance → MoA and AoA drafting → RJSC submission → Stamp-duty payment → Certificate of Incorporation.

Engagement covers: Handling name-clearance rejection rounds (typically one to two iterations on first preferences due to similarity hits); MoA and AoA vetting by RJSC officers with re-submission cycles on objects clauses; and stamp-duty calculation accuracy against authorised capital (a frequent over- or under-payment trigger).
notes:
Authority: Registrar of Joint Stock Companies and Firms (RJSC) · Karwan Bazar, Dhaka · roc.gov.bd
Legal basis: Companies Act, 1994 (latest amended by Companies (Second Amendment) Act, 2020)
Notes: RJSC fees vary by authorised capital. First annual return due within 18 months.

title: Factory license · DIFE application
description: End to end coordination for a new factory licence via the DIFE LIMA portal. Fire License and Environmental Clearance are required as prerequisites.
deliverables: Document prep incl. Fire and ECC prerequisites
workflow:
Process: Document prep incl. Fire and ECC prerequisites → LIMA portal Form 77 submission → DIFE site inspection → License issuance.

Engagement covers: Verifying that Fire License and ECC prerequisites are valid and live (commonly expired or in renewal queue, which is fatal to DIFE submission); managing LIMA portal technical issues that internal teams stall on; scheduling DIFE site inspection within the inspector's availability window; and handling one to two rounds of post-inspection remediation calls (worker register format, exit signage, first-aid kit positioning, and OSH register completeness are commonly cited).
notes:
Authority: Department of Inspection for Factories and Establishments (DIFE) · LIMA Portal · Issuing District Office
Legal basis: Bangladesh Labour Act, 2006 (latest amended 2026); Bangladesh Labour Rules, 2015 (latest amended 2022)
Notes: DIFE fees vary by worker headcount. Fire License & ECC must be in place first.

title: Factory license · DIFE renewal
description: Annual renewal filing on the DIFE LIMA portal. Updated supporting licences and treasury challan.
workflow:
Process: DIFE documentation update → LIMA portal submission → Inspection if required → Renewal.

Engagement covers: Ensuring DIFE annual filings (accident returns, workforce composition, welfare contributions) are submitted as prerequisites; managing the 31 December deadline; and absorbing officer turnover within DIFE district offices, which often causes file re-presentation delays after government fiscal-year transitions.
notes:
Authority: DIFE · LIMA Portal · Issuing District Office
Legal basis: Bangladesh Labour Act, 2006 (latest amended 2026); Bangladesh Labour Rules, 2015 (latest amended 2022)
Notes: Renewal must be completed before 31 December annually.

title: Factory layout plan approval
description: DIFE layout-plan approval for new factory construction or significant layout changes.
workflow:
Process: Layout drawing prep → DIFE submission → Site inspection → Approval.

Engagement covers: Coordination with the architectural firm to prepare drawings to DIFE technical standards; handling DIFE queries on machine spacing, worker movement paths, and welfare facility placement; and re-submission where site inspection flags variance from the approved drawing.
notes:
Authority: DIFE · LIMA Portal · Issuing District Office
Legal basis: Bangladesh Labour Act, 2006 (latest amended 2026); Bangladesh Labour Rules, 2015 (latest amended 2022)
Notes: Required prior to factory licence application for new factories.

title: Fire license · FSCD application
description: End to end coordination for a new fire licence from Fire Service and Civil Defence. Floor plan, inspection scheduling, and demand note payment.
deliverables: Fire safety documentation incl. FSCD-enlisted firm floor plan
workflow:
Process: Fire safety documentation incl. FSCD-enlisted firm floor plan → FSCD online and physical submission → Site inspection → Demand note → License issuance.

Engagement covers: Coordination with an FSCD-enlisted engineering firm for the floor plan certification (mandatory, only enlisted firms qualify); managing inspector site visit scheduling (depends on inspector availability, not yours); one to two rounds of remediation post-inspection (extinguisher expiry, exit-sign visibility, fire pump test status, hydrant pressure); demand-note review for fee accuracy; and ensuring the Treasury Challan deposit hits Code 1-2261-0000-2009 correctly. A wrong head means payment rejected and re-deposit, adding 5 to 10 days.
notes:
Authority: Bangladesh Fire Service & Civil Defence (FSCD) · Licensing Branch · HQ Kazi Alauddin Road, Dhaka
Legal basis: Fire Prevention and Extinction Act, 2003; Fire Prevention and Extinction Rules, 2014; Bangladesh National Building Code, 2020
Notes: Government fee calculated from FSCD demand note based on property valuation and combustible material.

title: Fire license · FSCD renewal
description: Annual renewal filing with FSCD before 30 June each fiscal year.
workflow:
Process: Original license and challan submission → FSCD inspector verification → Renewed license issuance.

Engagement covers: Ensuring renewal is filed before 30 June to avoid the 5% monthly penalty (which compounds for up to 6 months before automatic cancellation); managing inspector re-visit where the premises layout has changed since last renewal; and handling the original-license submission requirement, which is commonly misplaced by client teams and requires duplicate issuance from FSCD records first.
notes:
Authority: Bangladesh Fire Service & Civil Defence (FSCD) · Licensing Branch
Legal basis: Fire Prevention and Extinction Act, 2003; Rules, 2014; BNBC, 2020
Notes: On or before 30 June. Late incurs 5% monthly penalty up to 6 months before cancellation.

title: Fire safety plan · FSCD approval (NOC)
description: FSCD No Objection Certificate on the fire safety plan. Required before construction or significant modification.
deliverables: Fire-fighting floor plan by FSCD-enlisted firm
workflow:
Process: Fire-fighting floor plan by FSCD-enlisted firm → FSCD submission → Committee review → NOC issuance.

Engagement covers: Coordinating with the FSCD-enlisted firm on plan revisions; managing FSCD committee scheduling (fixed dates, so submission timing affects review window); and re-submission where the committee returns observations on hydrant placement, exit width, detector layout, or staircase pressurisation.
notes:
Authority: Bangladesh Fire Service & Civil Defence (FSCD) · Licensing Branch
Legal basis: Fire Prevention and Extinction Act, 2003; Rules, 2014; BNBC, 2020
Notes: Plan must be prepared by an FSCD-enlisted engineering firm.

title: Fire functionality certificate
description: Effectiveness certificate from FSCD confirming fire-safety arrangement is in place per the approved NOC. Required before fire-licence application.
workflow:
Process: Fire-safety installation verification → FSCD inspection committee → Standing committee review → Functionality certificate.

Engagement covers: Pre-inspection walk-through to identify gaps against the approved NOC; coordination with the installation contractor for remediation where gaps exist; managing the 90–120-day standing committee review cycle; and ensuring certificate issuance timing aligns with subsequent fire-licence application (a delay here cascades down the critical path).
notes:
Authority: Bangladesh Fire Service & Civil Defence (FSCD) · Licensing Branch
Legal basis: Fire Prevention and Extinction Act, 2003; Rules, 2014; BNBC, 2020
Notes: Longest fire-track service. Critical pathway item for factory commissioning.

title: Import registration · IRC
description: Coordination of new IRC application with the Chief Controller of Imports and Exports through a scheduled commercial bank.
workflow:
Process: Document collection → AD bank submission → CCI&E forwarding and review → IRC issuance.

Engagement covers: Selection of the correct AD bank branch (since some branches process IRC files faster than others); managing the bank's 3–7-day internal verification cycle before it forwards to CCI&E (a stage not reflected in CCI&E's published SOP); handling CCI&E queries on business activity versus HS codes claimed in the application; and setting the annual renewal calendar with the AD bank to avoid lapses.
notes:
Authority: Chief Controller of Imports and Exports (CCI&E) · Ministry of Commerce · TCB Bhaban, Karwan Bazar · routes via your nominated AD bank
Legal basis: Imports and Exports (Control) Act, 1950; Import Policy Order (current 2024–27); Export Policy Order (current 2024–27)
Notes: Routes through your nominated AD bank branch. Annual renewal required.

title: Export registration · ERC
description: Coordination of new ERC application with CCI&E through a scheduled commercial bank.
workflow:
Process: Document collection → AD bank submission → CCI&E forwarding and review → ERC issuance.

Engagement covers: Chamber membership verification as a mandatory upstream prerequisite; product or service category accuracy in the application (this affects downstream eligibility for export incentives); and managing AD bank verification timing.
notes:
Authority: CCI&E · Ministry of Commerce · TCB Bhaban, Karwan Bazar · routes via your nominated AD bank
Legal basis: Imports and Exports (Control) Act, 1950; Import / Export Policy Orders (current 2024–27)
Notes: Routes through your nominated AD bank branch. Annual renewal required.

title: Acid usage license
description: Application coordination for the acid-usage licence required for industrial users of regulated acids.
workflow:
Process: Application submission → District committee review and inspection → Certificate issuance.

Engagement covers: Preparing the justification for industrial acid use (the District Committee scrutinises against necessity rather than approving by default); site inspection coordination including secure storage arrangements; and managing the periodic re-justification requirement.
notes:
Authority: District Acid Control Committee (per applicant's district), under National Acid Control Council · MoHA
Legal basis: Acid Control Act, 2002; Acid Crime Prevention Act, 2002; Acid Control Rules, 2004
Notes: Required under Acid Control Act 2002 for industrial acid users.

title: Deep tubewell permission
description: Application coordination for deep tubewell groundwater extraction permission with the relevant water authority.
workflow:
Process: Water authority application → Site inspection → Approval.

Engagement covers: Identifying the correct authority for the project's hydrological zone (BWDB for most areas, BMDA in the north-west, WASA inside city limits); coordinating the hydrological survey where required; and managing seasonal scheduling constraints, since some authorities process applications on a quarterly committee basis.
notes:
Authority: BWDB Field Office (per location); BMDA for north-west zones; WASA inside service jurisdiction
Legal basis: Bangladesh Water Act, 2013; Groundwater Management Rules (where notified per zone)
Notes: Authority and conditions vary by hydrological zone.

title: Environmental clearance · ECC (new)
description: End-to-end coordination for a new Environmental Clearance Certificate from DoE, with category-appropriate documentation (Green / Orange A / Orange B / Red under ECR 2023).
deliverables: IEE or EIA report prep (where applicable)
workflow:
Process: Project categorisation → IEE or EIA report prep (where applicable) → DoE online submission → Site inspection → ECC issuance.

Engagement covers: Correct categorisation against ECR 2023 Schedule 1, since mis-categorisation triggers rejection or, worse, retrospective non-compliance; coordination with DoE-approved consultants for the IEE / EIA report; managing DoE Environmental Clearance Committee scheduling for Orange-B and Red categories (fixed-date meetings, so missing one adds a full cycle); and handling one to two rounds of committee observations before issuance.
notes:
Authority: Department of Environment (DoE) · Divisional Office (per project location), MoEFCC · ecc.doe.gov.bd
Legal basis: Bangladesh Environment Conservation Act, 1995 (latest amended 2010); Environment Conservation Rules, 2023 (replaced 1997 Rules)
Notes: Fee scales with project investment. Validity: Green 3 yrs; Orange A/B and Red 1 yr. Red requires EIA approval first.

title: Environmental clearance · ECC (renewal)
description: Renewal filing for Environmental Clearance Certificate. Initiated at least 30 days before expiry.
deliverables: Renewal submission with updated environmental data
workflow:
Process: Renewal submission with updated environmental data → DoE verification → Clearance renewal.

Engagement covers: Preparing the updated environmental performance data (effluent test results, air and noise quality logs, ETP operating records); managing the 30-days-before-expiry submission window; and handling DoE divisional officer queries on compliance with the conditions of the prior ECC.
notes:
Authority: Department of Environment (DoE) · Divisional Office · MoEFCC
Legal basis: Bangladesh Environment Conservation Act, 1995 (latest amended 2010); Environment Conservation Rules, 2023
Notes: Renewal fee is one-fourth of original ECC fee under Environment Conservation Rules.

title: NOC from local Pourashava
description: Coordination of No Objection Certificates from local Pourashava or Union Parishad. Used for fire license, environmental clearance, or other prerequisite filings.
workflow:
Process: Application drafting → Pourashava submission → Issuance.

Engagement covers: Identifying the responsible Pourashava or UP for the project location; drafting the NOC application aligned to the upstream filing it will support (fire-license NOC reads differently from ECC NOC); and managing local Councillor or Chairman sign-off where required, which is not always reflected in the official process.
notes:
Authority: Pourashava or Union Parishad (per project or business location)
Legal basis: Local Government (Pourashava) Act, 2009; Local Government (Union Parishad) Act, 2009
Notes: Lead time depends on local authority responsiveness.

title: Any other license · Custom scoping
description: Custom licensing work outside the standard catalog (BSTI, boiler, explosives, BTRC, sector-specific), scoped on request.
workflow:
Process: Need conversation → Authority identification → Written scope → Fixed-fee quote → Delivery.

Engagement covers: Up-front authority identification and a feasibility check before any commercial commitment.
notes:
Authority: Authority confirmed at scoping (BSTI, Chief Inspector of Boilers, Department of Explosives, BTRC, or sector-specific regulator)
Legal basis: Confirmed at scoping per the relevant sectoral Act and Rules
Notes: Catch-all entry for non-standard licensing work.

title: Trade license · Amendment
description: Filing of amendments to an existing trade licence (name, address, activity, or ownership) with the issuing City Corporation or Pourashava.
workflow:
Process: Amendment documentation → Authority submission → Approval.

Engagement covers: Handling City Corp officer queries on whether the change is material (some amendments trigger field inspection); and coordinating with the original holder of the licence if joint signatures are required.
notes:
Authority: DNCC or DSCC · Zonal Tax Office · e-Trade Portal where available, or applicable Pourashava
Legal basis: Local Government (City Corporation) Act, 2009; Pourashava Act, 2009; Trade License By-laws
Notes: Final cost depends on nature of amendment (BDT 5,000–35,000 range).

title: TIN certificate · Amendment
description: Filing of amendments to an existing company TIN certificate (address, name, RJSC details).
workflow:
Process: Document submission → NBR portal update → Amended certificate.

Engagement covers: Ensuring RJSC records match the requested change before submission (mismatches trigger rejection); portal-side verification rounds; and re-submission cycles where the amendment touches the entity's legal name (which often requires a fresh certificate rather than amendment).
notes:
Authority: NBR · e-TIN Portal (etaxnbr.gov.bd)
Legal basis: Income Tax Act, 2023; latest amended through Finance Ordinance, 2025
Notes: No government fee for amendment.

title: DIFE credentials · Update
description: LIMA portal credential update for DIFE. Authorised user changes, employer information corrections, and contact updates.
workflow:
Process: Portal login update → Employer info correction → DIFE approval.

Engagement covers: Identifying the correct LIMA sub-form for the specific credential change; managing the District Office officer-approval requirement (sometimes bypassed by the portal but still required in practice); and ensuring downstream filings can continue while the credential update is pending.
notes:
Authority: DIFE · LIMA Portal · Issuing District Office
Legal basis: Bangladesh Labour Act, 2006 (latest amended 2026); Bangladesh Labour Rules, 2015 (latest amended 2022)
Notes: No standard government fee.

title: CCI&E credentials · Update
description: Portal credential update for CCI&E. Authorised signatory and contact changes.
workflow:
Process: Portal update → CCI&E verification → Approval.

Engagement covers: Managing the AD bank notification requirement that is commonly missed by internal teams; and ensuring authorised-signatory updates align with bank records to avoid downstream IRC and ERC routing failures.
notes:
Authority: CCI&E · Ministry of Commerce · TCB Bhaban · routes via AD bank
Legal basis: Imports and Exports (Control) Act, 1950; Import / Export Policy Orders (2024–27)
Notes: No standard government fee.

title: BIDA registration · Amendment
description: Filing of amendments to an existing BIDA company or office registration. Ownership, capital, address, or activity changes.
workflow:
Process: Amendment documentation → BIDA OSS submission → Inter-ministerial review where required → Approval.

Engagement covers: Determining up-front whether the amendment qualifies as a simple amendment or triggers fresh registration review (capital, ownership, and activity changes often trigger the latter); and coordinating with Bangladesh Bank for foreign capital movement reporting where applicable.
notes:
Authority: BIDA · Registration Wing · OSS Portal
Legal basis: BIDA Act, 2016; Guideline 2011 for Branch, Liaison and Representative Office Registration
Notes: Material amendments may trigger fresh review.

title: BIDA OSS credentials · Update
description: BIDA OSS portal credential and access correction. Authorised user and login restoration.
workflow:
Process: Portal access correction request → BIDA verification → Approval.

Engagement covers: Verifying the authorised user mandate from the company board; managing the BIDA OSS support desk turnaround; and ensuring downstream services (work permit filings and renewals) can resume immediately on approval.
notes:
Authority: BIDA One Stop Service (OSS) Portal · Agargaon HQ
Legal basis: Bangladesh Investment Development Authority Act, 2016
Notes: No standard government fee.
